chunk_summary: This section focuses on balanced growth assessment in preterm, LBW, and ELBW infants post-discharge, emphasizing weight, length, and head circumference tracking to prevent growth faltering.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of balanced growth assessment using weight, length, and head circumference in preterm infants post-discharge.
Identify strategies to prevent growth faltering in preterm and LBW infants transitioning from NICU to home.
Learn about the role of breast milk fortifiers and enteral nutrition in supporting post-discharge growth.
Familiarize with updated guidelines for post-discharge nutrition in preterm infants.
key_facts: Growth faltering is a critical concern in preterm and LBW infants post-discharge, requiring close monitoring of weight, length, and head circumference.
The ESPGHAN 2022 guidelines emphasize the importance of enteral nutrition in preterm infants for optimal growth and development.
Breast milk fortifiers are recommended for preterm infants to meet their higher nutritional needs post-discharge (East of England Neonatal Network, 2023).
Clinical Protocol #12 by the Academy of Breastfeeding Medicine provides guidance on transitioning preterm infants from NICU to home.
Regular growth assessments should align with standardized growth charts specific to preterm and LBW infants.
reference_quote: The ESPGHAN 2022 guidelines emphasize the importance of enteral nutrition in preterm infants for optimal growth and development.
document_key_topics: Prevention of growth faltering in preterm infants
Role of breast milk fortifiers and enteral nutrition
Guidelines for post-discharge nutrition in preterm infants
